connectWallet.title: Connect Your Wallet

register.formDescription: Configure your professional profile and security deposit

requirements.title: Agent Requirements
requirements.stablecoinStake: Stablecoin stake determines your Maximum Arbitratable Value (MAV = 20× stake)
requirements.daoTokenStake: DAO token stake required for protocol alignment (can be slashed for misconduct)
requirements.responseDeadline: Respond to assigned disputes within the deadline (7 days by default)
requirements.fairResolution: Fair and impartial dispute resolution expected

register.assignmentFeeHelper: Charged automatically upon case assignment. <earn>Earn ${amount} on a $10k escrow.</earn>
register.disputeFeeHelper: Earned only upon successful case resolution. <earn>Earn ${amount} on a $10k escrow.</earn>
register.bioPlaceholder: Expert in DeFi, NFT Royalties, and Cross-chain bridges...
register.bytesTip: Tip: some characters (like "→", emojis, etc.) take more than 1 byte and may exceed the on-chain limit.
register.securityApprovalsDesc: Grant permission to lock collateral tokens in the registry
register.termsAcknowledgement: I acknowledge that my stake serves as bond and can be slashed for protocol misconduct as defined in the <link>Agent Terms & Conditions</link>.
register.enterAmountToApprove: Enter an amount to enable approval

success.title: Registration Successful!
success.description: You are now registered as an agent. You can start accepting disputes.

alreadyRegistered.title: Already Registered
alreadyRegistered.description: Your wallet is already registered as an agent.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.mavAnswer: MAV determines the maximum escrow value you can be assigned to resolve. It's calculated as 20× your stablecoin stake. For example, a $100 stake gives you a $2,000 MAV.
faq.feesQuestion: What are the two fees for?
faq.feesAnswer: <bold>Assignment Fee:</bold> Charged when you're assigned to an escrow (even without disputes). <bold>Dispute Fee:</bold> Charged only when you resolve a dispute. Both are percentages of the escrow amount.
faq.slashQuestion: When can my stake be slashed?
faq.slashAnswer: Your DAO token stake can be slashed by DAO governance if you fail to respond to disputes within the deadline or if misconduct is determined through a governance proposal.
faq.withdrawAnswer: Yes, you can unstake when you have no active cases. First, set yourself as unavailable, then wait 30 days (engagement cooldown), after which you can execute the unstake to withdraw both stakes.
faq.gaslessAnswer: Tokens that support EIP-2612 permit allow you to sign an approval message instead of sending a separate transaction. This saves gas costs. USDC and the DAO token support this feature; USDT does not.

withdraw.description: Manage your retirement and withdraw available collateral
withdraw.activeDescription: You are currently accepting new disputes. To withdraw your stake, you must first disable availability.
withdraw.retiringDescription: Availability disabled. You are no longer assigned to new cases.
withdraw.cooldownExplanation: The security cooldown ensures that no malicious actions were taken before exiting the protocol. Once all conditions are met, the button above will enable.

setAvailable.title: Set Available
setAvailable.message: You will start receiving new dispute invitations again.

setUnavailable.title: Set Unavailable
setUnavailable.message:
While unavailable:
• You will not receive new dispute invitations.
• Any escrow that selected you as agent cannot invite you during a dispute.
• Parties may be forced to negotiate a settlement (SPLIT) without your decision.
{casesLine}
This may reduce trust from parties who chose you to arbitrate.
